{"toolCallId":"toolu_01FDU6xoYbUcCCdfaaAnopP4","toolName":"read","resultJson":"{\"success\":true,\"message\":\"File content:\",\"content\":\"1: # Sterownik Akwarium PRO v5.1\\n2: \\n3: Dokumentacja tego pliku jest zaktualizowana pod aktualny kod w folderze `src/`.\\n4: \\n5: ## 1. Co robi projekt\\n6: \\n7: Sterownik pracuje na ESP32-S3 (FreeRTOS, dual core) i obsluguje:\\n8: \\n9: - harmonogram swiatla, filtra i napowietrzania\\n10: - pomiar temperatury DS18B20 + sterowanie grzalka (histereza + ograniczenie przelaczen)\\n11: - karmnik automatyczny i reczny (z czujnikiem i timeoutem)\\n12: - pomiar napiecia baterii RTC CR2032\\n13: - lokalny panel WWW + OTA przez upload pliku `.bin`\\n14: - lokalny interfejs OLED 128x32 + przyciski fizyczne\\n15: - logi systemowe (RAM + trwale logi krytyczne w Preferences)\\n16: - usypianie ekranu i Deep Sleep\\n17: \\n18: ## 2. Architektura (aktualna)\\n19: \\n20: Najwazniejsze moduly:\\n21: \\n22: - `Akwarium.ino` - setup, loop i UI state machine\\n23: - `SystemController.*` - glowna logika sterowania i decyzje runtime\\n24: - `AkwariumWifi.*` - WiFi STA/AP, HTTP server, captive portal DNS, OTA upload endpoint\\n25: - `ApiHandlers.*` - endpointy `/api/status`, `/api/logs`, `/api/action`\\n26: - `ConfigManager.*` + `ConfigData.h` - konfiguracja w Preferences (CRC32)\\n27: - `ScheduleManager.*` - okna czasowe i auto-feeding\\n28: - `PowerManager.*` + `BatteryReader.*` - bateria, aktywnosc, tryby zasilania\\n29: - `TemperatureController.*` - DS18B20 i grzalka\\n30: - `FeederController.*` - silnik karmnika + logika czujnika\\n31: - `ServoController.*` - serwo napowietrzania (attach tylko na czas ruchu)\\n32: - `SharedState.*` - bezpieczny snapshot danych miedzy rdzeniami\\n33: - `AquariumAnimation.*` + `UIRenderers.*` - OLED/UI\\n34: \\n35: ## 3. Piny (z kodu)\\n36: \\n37: ```\\n38: BUTTON_UP_PIN      GPIO15\\n39: BUTTON_SELECT_PIN  GPIO16\\n40: BUTTON_DOWN_PIN    GPIO14\\n41: \\n42: ONE_WIRE_BUS       GPIO1   (DS18B20)\\n43: HEATER_PIN         GPIO3\\n44: PUMP_PIN           GPIO4\\n45: FEEDER_PIN         GPIO5\\n46: SERVO_PIN          GPIO6\\n47: \\n48: BAT_ADC_PIN        GPIO7\\n49: BAT_EN_PIN         GPIO10\\n50: FEEDER_SENSOR_PIN  GPIO12\\n51: LIGHT_PIN          GPIO17\\n52: ```\\n53: \\n54: Uwagi:\\n55: \\n56: - `LIGHT_PIN` jest aktywny w logice odwrotnej (`LOW = ON`, `HIGH = OFF`).\\n57: - `PUMP_PIN` i `HEATER_PIN` dzialaja klasycznie (`HIGH = ON`).\\n58: \\n59: ## 4. Build i upload (PlatformIO)\\n60: \\n61: Aktualne srodowisko w `platformio.ini`:\\n62: \\n63: - `env:esp32-s3-devkitc-1`\\n64: - `platform = espressif32`\\n65: - `framework = arduino`\\n66: \\n67: Przyklady:\\n68: \\n69: ```bash\\n70: pio run\\n71: pio run -t upload\\n72: pio device monitor\\n73: ```\\n74: \\n75: ## 5. Konfiguracja WiFi/AP\\n76: \\n77: W `src/arduino_secrets.h` ustaw:\\n78: \\n79: - `SECRET_SSID`, `SECRET_PASS` - domowa siec WiFi (tryb STA)\\n80: - `AP_SSID`, `AP_PASSWORD` - dane Access Point urzadzenia\\n81: \\n82: Start systemu:\\n83: \\n84: - urzadzenie probuje polaczyc sie jako STA przez 6 s\\n85: - po timeout pracuje offline (bez automatycznego AP)\\n86: - AP uruchamia sie recznie z menu `Wifi`\\n87: \\n88: ## 6. Web panel i API\\n89: \\n90: HTTP:\\n91: \\n92: - `GET /` - panel WWW\\n93: - `GET /style.css`, `GET /script.js` - assety\\n94: - `POST /settime?epoch=<unix>` - synchronizacja czasu RTC\\n95: - `POST /update` - OTA upload firmware\\n96: \\n97: REST API:\\n98: \\n99: - `GET /api/status` - status urzadzenia (temp, bateria, przekazniki, serwo, harmonogram, siec)\\n100: - `GET /api/logs` - logi normalne + krytyczne\\n101: - `POST /api/action` - akcje sterujace\\n102: \\n103: Dozwolone akcje `action`:\\n104: \\n105: - `feed_now`\\n106: - `set_servo` (wymaga `angle` 0..90)\\n107: - `clear_servo`\\n108: - `clear_critical_logs`\\n109: - `save_schedule` (parametry harmonogramu i temperatury)\\n110: \\n111: ## 7. UI i przyciski\\n112: \\n113: Menu glowne (OLED): `Harmonogramy`, `Logi`, `Data i Czas`, `Test`, `Wifi`.\\n114: \\n115: Skrot zachowania:\\n116: \\n117: - HOME: `SELECT` -> MENU\\n118: - MENU: `DOWN` nastepna pozycja, `SELECT` wejscie, `UP` powrot\\n119: - Ekrany harmonogramow / czasu: `SELECT` edycja/nastepne pole, `DOWN` zmiana wartosci\\n120: - LOGI: `DOWN` przewijanie\\n121: - Wifi/AP: `UP` reczne wylaczenie AP i powrot do menu\\n122: \\n123: ## 8. Zasilanie i sleep\\n124: \\n125: - po 4 min bez aktywnosci ekran przechodzi w power-save (jesli `alwaysScreenOn == false`)\\n126: - po 5 min bez aktywnosci i przy spelnieniu warunkow system wchodzi w Deep Sleep na 30 min\\n127: - wybudzanie: przyciski (`ESP_EXT1_WAKEUP_ANY_LOW`) lub timer RTC\\n128: \\n129: Warunki Deep Sleep (w skrocie):\\n130: \\n131: - grzalka wylaczona\\n132: - OTA nie jest w trakcie\\n133: - AP wylaczony i brak aktywnego polaczenia STA\\n134: \\n135: ## 9. Domyslna konfiguracja\\n136: \\n137: Domyslne wartosci z `ConfigManager::loadDefaultConfig()`:\\n138: \\n139: - dzien: `10:00 -> 21:30`\\n140: - napowietrzanie: `10:00 -> 21:00`\\n141: - filtr: `10:30 -> 20:30`\\n142: - temperatura docelowa: `25.0 C`\\n143: - histereza temperatury: `0.5 C`\\n144: - karmienie: `18:00`, tryb `1` (codziennie)\\n145: - `servoPreOffMins = 30`\\n146: - `alwaysScreenOn = false`\\n147: \\n148: ## 10. Logi i diagnostyka\\n149: \\n150: - logi normalne: ring buffer 20 wpisow (RAM)\\n151: - logi krytyczne: ring buffer 20 wpisow (trwale, Preferences)\\n152: - czyszczenie logow krytycznych: `POST /api/action` z `action=clear_critical_logs`\\n153: \\n154: ## 11. Bezpieczenstwo runtime\\n155: \\n156: - brak poprawnego odczytu DS18B20 (seria bledow) -> fail-safe, grzalka OFF\\n157: - twardy limit temperatury: przy `>= 28.0 C` grzalka jest natychmiast odcinana\\n158: - manualny override serwa wygasa automatycznie po 5 minutach\\n159: - karmnik ma timeout bezpieczenstwa (`15 s`) i logike cyklu czujnika\\n160: \"}","providerOptions":{}}

{"text":"Now I understand the project. It's an aquarium controller (Sterownik Akwarium PRO v5.1) running on ESP32-S3. I need to create a mobile app UI for controlling/monitoring this aquarium controller.

The app should have:
1. **Dashboard/Status** - temperature, battery, relays status, schedule, network
2. **Schedule Management** - light, aeration, filter, feeding schedule
3. **Temperature Control** - target temp, hysteresis
4. **Feeder Control** - manual feeding, schedule
5. **Logs** - normal logs, critical logs


6. **WiFi/Network** - WiFi status, AP control
7. **Settings** - configuration

The API provides endpoints for device status, logs, and control actions like feeding, servo control, and schedule management. I'm building a mobile-first React app with multiple screens using React Router to simulate the aquarium controller interface, starting with a dashboard that displays the current temperature, relay states, battery level, and upcoming scheduled tasks.

I'll organize the app into screens for the schedule, manual feeder control, system logs, and configuration settings. The design will use a dark aquarium-themed palette with blues and teals, a bottom navigation bar for easy mobile access, and mock data for testing. Now I need to set up React Router and check the project dependencies.","signature":"","providerOptions":{}}

{"text":"Great, I have all the packages I need. Let me now create a comprehensive mobile UI for the aquarium controller. I'll use React Router for navigation.

Let me plan the structure:
- `/src/app/App.tsx` - main with RouterProvider
- `/src/app/routes.ts` - routes
- `/src/app/components/Layout.tsx` - mobile layout with bottom nav
- `/src/app/pages/Dashboard.tsx` - home screen with status overview
- `/src/app/pages/Schedule.tsx` - schedule management
- `/src/app/pages/Feeder.tsx` - feeder control


- `/src/app/pages/Logs.tsx` - system logs
- `/src/app/pages/Settings.tsx` - settings & config

I'll design this with a dark aquarium theme using blues and teals, populate it with mock data, and build out cards for device status, toggle switches for relays, time pickers for schedules, and temperature charts using recharts. I'm settling on a dark navy background with slightly lighter cards and teal accents for the color scheme.

{"text":"Now I have a good understanding of the project. Let me create a comprehensive mobile UI for the aquarium controller. I'll plan the app structure:

1. **App.tsx** - RouterProvider
2. **routes.ts** - routes config
3. **components/Layout.tsx** - mobile shell with bottom navigation
4. **pages/Dashboard.tsx** - main status overview
5. **pages/Schedule.tsx** - schedule management (light, filter, aeration, feeding)
6. **pages/Feeder.tsx** - feeder control + manual feeding
7. **pages/Logs.tsx** - normal + critical logs


8. **pages/Settings.tsx** - settings (temperature, servo, WiFi, OTA)

I'm going with a dark aquarium-themed design using deep blue backgrounds with teal accents, glowing effects for active states, status cards with icons, a temperature chart, toggle switches for relay control, and bottom tab navigation. Now I'll start building out the components with a color palette of very dark blue backgrounds and darker card colors.

Switching to Tailwind's built-in dark palette instead—using slate-950 for the main background, slate-900 for cards, slate-800 for inputs, and cyan-400 for the primary accent color, with green-400 for active states and red-400 for warnings. For the mock data, I'm setting up temperature readings at 24.8°C with a target of 25°C and battery status.

Building the Settings component... Setting up the context structure... Finishing up the data structure with logs and system info. Now I'm moving on to build the mobile interface—I'll create a centered 390x844px container matching iPhone 14 dimensions, then set up a bottom navigation with five tabs for the dashboard, schedule, feeder controls, logs, and settings.

For the main dashboard, I'm planning a header with the aquarium name and current time, a prominent temperature gauge display, battery status indicator, four control tiles for the major systems with toggles, a feed-now button, upcoming scheduled events, and a mini temperature chart showing the last 12 hours of data. The schedule screen will have tabs for each device type with time range selectors and enable/disable toggles. The feeder section is next...

Building the mobile interface...","signature":"","providerOptions":{}}
